message:
refactor(storage): consolidate atomic writes to util.AtomicWriteFile

Replaces inline atomic write patterns (temp file + rename) with the
centralized util.AtomicWriteFile() helper across four storage modules.

Changes by file:
- checkpoint/storage.go: SaveScrollback, SaveGitPatch, SaveGitStatus,
  and writeJSON now use util.AtomicWriteFile instead of duplicated
  temp-file-then-rename logic. Removes ~25 lines of boilerplate.
- history/storage.go: Prune() and PruneByTime() rewritten to use
  bytes.Buffer then single atomic write. Also optimizes scanner buffer
  allocation (start at 64KB instead of 5MB, grow as needed).
- session/storage.go: Save() simplified from manual temp file handling
  to single util.AtomicWriteFile call.
- cli/setup.go: writeDefaultSetupConfig and writeDefaultSetupPolicy
  use atomic writes for crash-safe config creation.

Benefits:
- Single source of truth for atomic write semantics
- Consistent error handling and cleanup across all storage paths
- Reduced code duplication (~80 lines removed)
- Easier to maintain and audit for correctness

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 `

+	return util.AtomicWriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644)
 }

 `
-	return os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644)
+	return util.AtomicWriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644)
